senses.definition: to dispatch again or at a later time after an initial dispatch has occurred
examples: The crew decided to redispatch the cargo to a later flight.
After a delay, the company chose to redispatch the shipment to the original destination.

senses.definition: to drive away or banish again; to expel once more
examples: The council attempted to redispel dissenters from the area.
Efforts were made to redispel the stray group from the park.

senses.definition: the act or process of dispersing again or spreading out after a prior concentration
examples: The redispersal of seeds after rainfall helped with germination.
Scientists studied the redispersal pattern of particulates.

senses.definition: to disperse again or spread out after clustering
examples: Microparticles redispersed when the solvent warmed.
The team observed how the sample redispersed under agitation.

senses.definition: having been dispersed again after clumping or settling
examples: The pigment was redispersed in water to form a uniform slurry.
Redispersed particles produced a stable colloidal solution.

senses.definition: the action of dispersing again after settling or clumping
examples: Stirring helped in redispersing the settled particles.
Redispersing the colloid requires gentle agitation.

senses.definition: to display again or show again to an audience or user
examples: The librarian chose to redisplay the popular titles.
The museum will redisplay the exhibit after renovations.

senses.definition: having been displayed again
examples: The gallery has redisplayed the portraits after restoration.
The software function redisplayed the saved images.

senses.definition: displaying again or showing again to an audience or user
examples: The monitor is redisplaying the last session's screenshots.
The mall screens are redisplaying advertisements.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of redisplay; displays again
examples: The gallery redisplays the classics every season.
The screen redisplays the latest update.

senses.definition: to place or arrange anew for a particular purpose or attitude
examples: The speaker tried to redispose the audience toward optimism.
The gardener will redispose the plants for better sunlight.

senses.definition: having been placed or arranged anew
examples: The committee redisposed the seating to improve visibility.
The items were redisposed after the break.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of redispose; places anew
examples: The curator redisposes the artifacts for the exhibit.
The layout redisposes the furniture in the hall.

senses.definition: the act of placing or arranging anew with intent
examples: The planner is redisposing elements to improve flow.
They are redisposing the landscape to create better sightlines.

senses.definition: the act or result of rearranging or placing again
examples: The redisposition of the files improved access time.
A careful redisposition of chapters aided comprehension.

senses.definition: plural of redisposition; acts or results of rearranging anew
examples: The redispositions of the furniture changed the room's dynamics.
Policy redispositions can affect budget allocations.

senses.definition: to dispute again or reassert a disagreement
examples: The two parties decided to redispute the terms of the contract.
Scientists may redispute prior conclusions with new data.

senses.definition: having been disputed again or reargued
examples: The issue was redisputed after revised data emerged.
They redisputed the licensing terms.

senses.definition: the act of disputing again or rearguing
examples: The negotiators are redisputing the terms tonight.
Researchers are redisputing the hypothesis with new data.

senses.definition: to dissect again, or examine in detail anew
examples: The scientist redissected the specimen to verify results.
They redissect the data to extract new insights.

senses.definition: the act of dissecting again or examining in detail anew
examples: The redissection of the document revealed hidden clauses.
A careful redissection of the data uncovered anomalies.

senses.definition: a nonstandard or erroneous term; no established dictionary sense
examples: The term redisseise appears to be a misspelling; no standard sense is recognized.
If this is a specialized term, please provide context.

senses.definition: unrecognized token; no established sense in contemporary English
examples: Redisseisin does not match known English vocabulary.

senses.definition: unrecognized token; no established sense in contemporary English
examples: Redisseisor is not found in standard lexicons.

senses.definition: to seize again; to take hold of something anew
examples: The authorities moved to redisseize the illicit goods.
They attempted to redisseize control over the project.
